How it works

01

### Surveillance system

A single `SurveillanceSystem_v9` R6 object registers all database tables
and tasks, giving the whole pipeline one place to configure and run.

02

### Tasks, plans, analyses

Work is arranged in a three-level hierarchy — tasks contain plans, plans
contain analyses — so data is pulled once per plan and reused across all
analyses inside it, with optional parallel execution across plans.

03

### Execution logging

Every task run is recorded via
[`update_config_log()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/update_config_log.md),
and
[`get_config_tasks_stats()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/get_config_tasks_stats.md)
returns timing and status so you can diagnose failures and track
pipeline performance over time.

## Overview

[Core Surveillance 9](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/) (“cs9”) is a free
and open-source framework for real-time analysis and disease
surveillance.

Read the introduction vignette
[here](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/articles/cs9.html) or run
[`help(package="cs9")`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference).

# Package index

## Core Classes

Main R6 classes for surveillance system architecture

- [`SurveillanceSystem_v9`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/SurveillanceSystem_v9.md)
  : A Surveillance System Object
- [`TaskJob`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/TaskJob.md) :
  TaskJob R6 Class

## Configuration Management

Functions for logging and configuration tracking

- [`config`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/config.md) :
  Flags/values to be used in the 'dashboards' scene
- [`update_config_log()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/update_config_log.md)
  : Update Configuration Log
- [`get_config_log()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/get_config_log.md)
  : Get Configuration Log
- [`get_config_data_hash_for_each_plan()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/get_config_data_hash_for_each_plan.md)
  : Get Configuration Data Hash for Each Plan
- [`get_config_tables_last_updated()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/get_config_tables_last_updated.md)
  : Get Configuration Tables Last Updated
- [`get_config_tasks_stats()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/get_config_tasks_stats.md)
  : Get Configuration Tasks Statistics

## Environment Setup

Environment validation and diagnostic functions

- [`check_environment_setup()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/check_environment_setup.md)
  : Check Environment Setup

## File and Path Management

Utilities for managing directories and file paths

- [`path()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/path.md) : Get
  Results Folder Path
- [`create_folder_if_doesnt_exist()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/create_folder_if_doesnt_exist.md)
  : Create Folder If It Doesn't Exist
- [`create_latest_folder()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/create_latest_folder.md)
  : Create Latest Folder

## Data Processing Utilities

Helper functions for data manipulation and filtering

- [`mandatory_db_filter()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/mandatory_db_filter.md)
  : Filter surveillance data by standard epidemiological dimensions

## Task Management

Functions for running and managing surveillance tasks

- [`run_task_sequentially_as_rstudio_job_using_load_all()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/run_task_sequentially_as_rstudio_job_using_load_all.md)
  : Run a Task Sequentially as an RStudio Job
- [`run_task_sequentially_as_callr_bg_using_load_all()`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/run_task_sequentially_as_callr_bg_using_load_all.md)
  : Run a cs9 task in a background process, streaming its output live

## Imported Functions

Re-exported functions from other packages

- [`reexports`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/reexports.md)
  [`%>%`](https://niphr.github.io/cs9/reference/reexports.md) : Objects
  exported from other packages
